request.question: Replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant in the freezing medium will increase post-thaw viability of HeLa cells by at least 15 percentage points compared to the standard DMSO protocol, due to trehalose's superior membrane stabilization at low temperatures.

hypothesis.intervention: Replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant in the freezing medium
hypothesis.threshold: at least 15 percentage points higher than DMSO control
hypothesis.mechanistic_rationale: due to trehalose's superior membrane stabilization at low temperatures
hypothesis.readiness_rationale: All core fields are present and specific, making the hypothesis CRO-ready today.
hypothesis.literature_search_hint: trehalose sucrose cryoprotectant HeLa post-thaw viability
hypothesis.original_hypothesis: Replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant in the freezing medium will increase post-thaw viability of HeLa cells by at least 15 percentage points compared to the standard DMSO protocol, due to trehalose's superior membrane stabilization at low temperatures.

The papers focus on different biological systems or interventions, such as sperm cryopreservation, which does not align with the proposed hypothesis.

plan.objective: To determine the effect of trehalose as a cryoprotectant on post-thaw viability of HeLa cells compared to DMSO.
plan.experimental_design: HeLa cells will be cryopreserved using two different cryoprotectants: trehalose and DMSO. Each treatment will be replicated three times to ensure statistical validity. Post-thaw viability will be assessed using trypan blue exclusion after a 30-minute recovery period at 37°C.
controls: HeLa cells cryopreserved with 10% DMSO in freezing medium.
HeLa cells without any cryoprotectant in freezing medium.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 48 h | Cell Culture
Expand HeLa cells in 75 cm² flasks using DMEM supplemented with 10% FBS and 1% penicillin-streptomycin at 37°C, 5% CO2 until reaching 80% confluency (approximately 48 h).
Equipment: Thermo Scientific Heracell 150i incubator, Corning 75 cm² cell culture flasks
Reagents: DMEM (Gibco, 11965-092), Fetal Bovine Serum (FBS, Sigma, F7524), Penicillin-Streptomycin (Gibco, 15140-122)
Expected outcome: Cells reach 80% confluency in 48 h.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 1 h | Cryoprotectant Preparation
Prepare trehalose solution at 200 mM in sterile PBS. Prepare DMSO solution at 10% (v/v) in sterile PBS. Filter sterilize both solutions using 0.22 µm filters.
Equipment: Sartorius Stedim Biotech 0.22 µm filter, Sterile 50 mL centrifuge tubes
Reagents: 200 mM trehalose (Sigma, T9531), 10% DMSO (Sigma, D2650), PBS (Gibco, 14190-094)
Expected outcome: Sterile trehalose and DMSO solutions are prepared.

DAY 1 | 30 min | Cell Harvesting
Detach HeLa cells using 0.25% trypsin-EDTA solution. Neutralize with complete DMEM and centrifuge at 300 × g for 5 min at room temperature. Resuspend cells in freezing medium.
Equipment: Eppendorf 5424 centrifuge, Corning 50 mL centrifuge tubes
Reagents: 0.25% Trypsin-EDTA (Gibco, 25200-056), Complete DMEM (DMEM + 10% FBS + 1% penicillin-streptomycin)
Expected outcome: Cells are detached, counted, and resuspended in freezing medium.

DAY 1 | 1 h | Cryopreservation
Aliquot 1 mL of cell suspension (1 x 10^6 cells/mL) into two cryovials for each treatment: one with trehalose and one with DMSO. For trehalose, mix 900 µL of cell suspension with 100 µL of 200 mM trehalose. For DMSO, mix 900 µL of cell suspension with 100 µL of 10% DMSO. Place vials in a freezing container (Mr. Frosty) at -80°C for 24 h.
Equipment: Mr. Frosty freezing container, Cryovials (Nunc)
Expected outcome: Cells are successfully cryopreserved at -80°C.

DAY 2 | 10 min | Thawing
Remove cryovials from -80°C and quickly thaw in a 37°C water bath for 2-3 min until just thawed. Immediately transfer to 10 mL of complete DMEM to dilute cryoprotectant. Centrifuge at 300 × g for 5 min at room temperature.
Equipment: 37°C water bath, Eppendorf 5424 centrifuge
Expected outcome: Cells are thawed and diluted in complete DMEM.

DAY 2 | 30 min | Post-Thaw Recovery
Incubate the cells in complete DMEM at 37°C for 30 min to recover from cryopreservation stress before viability assessment.
Equipment: Thermo Scientific Heracell 150i incubator
Expected outcome: Cells recover for 30 min at 37°C.

DAY 2 | 30 min | Viability Assessment
Count viable and non-viable cells using trypan blue exclusion method. Mix 10 µL of cell suspension with 10 µL of 0.4% trypan blue, load onto a hemocytometer, and count under a microscope. Calculate viability percentage.
Equipment: Countess II FL Automated Cell Counter, Hemocytometer
Reagents: 0.4% Trypan Blue (Gibco, 15250-061)
Expected outcome: Viability percentage is calculated for each treatment.

DAY 2 | 1 h | Data Analysis
Compare post-thaw viability percentages between trehalose and DMSO treatments using a two-tailed t-test. Set significance level at p < 0.05.
Expected outcome: Statistical significance is determined between treatments.

DAY 2 | 2 d | Replication
Repeat the entire cryopreservation and viability assessment process two additional times to ensure replicability of results.
Expected outcome: Results are consistent across three independent replicates.

DAY 2 | 1 h | Final Reporting
Compile data from all replicates, including mean viability percentages and standard deviations for both treatments. Prepare a report summarizing findings and implications.
Expected outcome: Comprehensive report detailing experimental findings is completed.

assumptions: Trehalose will provide superior membrane stabilization compared to DMSO during cryopreservation.
HeLa cells will respond similarly to trehalose as observed in other cell types.
The trypan blue exclusion method will accurately reflect cell viability post-thaw.
decision_criteria: Proceed if post-thaw viability with trehalose is at least 15 percentage points higher than DMSO control.
Proceed if all replicates show consistent viability results within ±5% of the mean.
Proceed if statistical analysis shows p < 0.05 between treatments.
reproducibility_notes: Use the same batch of HeLa cells for all experiments to minimize variability.
Standardize freezing and thawing rates using the same Mr. Frosty container.
Ensure all reagents are from the same lot number to reduce variability.
feedback_incorporated:
PRIOR SCIENTIST CORRECTIONS FOR SIMILAR EXPERIMENTS:
A scientist has previously flagged these specific issues on similar comparative_cryopreservation experiments. You MUST implement each correction below in your output unless it directly contradicts the current hypothesis:
1. replicate gate in plan text
2. add 30-minute post-thaw recovery at 37°C before trypan blue counting
3. change one

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: at least 15 percentage points higher than DMSO control improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: To determine the effect of trehalose as a cryoprotectant on post-thaw viability of HeLa cells compared to DMSO.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
cro_ready_brief.materials_responsibility: CRO to quote and confirm all materials before ordering.
questions_for_cro: Can you support controlled-rate freezing logs in each run?
Can you include replicate-level batch metadata in the final report?
